items.title: A cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz targeted by Iran
items.standfirst: The US military says it has carried out strikes on Iran in response to a drone attack on a cargo ship, after President Donald Trump blamed Tehran for the incident in the Strait of Hormuz and described it as a "foolish violation" of the ceasefire. The escalation comes at a particularly delicate moment, as Washington and Tehran continue negotiations aimed at securing an interim peace agreement. Caitlin Brown and Marion Meyer report.
items.content: The US military says it has carried out strikes on Iran in response to a drone attack on a cargo ship, after President Donald Trump blamed Tehran for the incident in the Strait of Hormuz and described it as a "foolish violation" of the ceasefire. The escalation comes at a particularly delicate moment, as Washington and Tehran continue negotiations aimed at securing an interim peace agreement. Caitlin Brown and Marion Meyer report.

items.title: JD Vance says, "Violence will be met with violence" after Iran strikes
items.standfirst: The U.S. launched a new round of air strikes one day after an Iranian attack on a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz. U.S. Central Command said the strikes targeted missile and drone storage locations as well as coastal radar sites. CBS News national security contributor Sam Vinograd has more.

items.title: US says it struck Iran targets after attack on cargo ship in the strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: Strikes against military facilities were in response to drone attack a day earlier on a cargo vesselThe US has struck Iran in a tit-for-tat response to a drone strike on a cargo ship, as the ceasefire between the US and Iran that reopened the strait of Hormuz undergoes its greatest test yet.The US strikes targeted multiple missile and drone facilities in Iran near the strait of Hormuz and on Qeshm Island on Friday in what appeared to be a limited strike meant to respond to Iran’s attack on a Singapore-flagged cargo ship without escalating the conflict. Continue reading...
items.content: Strikes against military facilities were in response to drone attack a day earlier on a cargo vesselThe US has struck Iran in a tit-for-tat response to a drone strike on a cargo ship, as the ceasefire between the US and Iran that reopened the strait of Hormuz undergoes its greatest test yet.The US strikes targeted multiple missile and drone facilities in Iran near the strait of Hormuz and on Qeshm Island on Friday in what appeared to be a limited strike meant to respond to Iran’s attack on a Singapore-flagged cargo ship without escalating the conflict. items.title: Middle East war live: Iran accuses US of 'blatant violation' of peace deal after latest strikes
items.standfirst: Iran on Saturday accused the United States of a "blatant violation" of the peace deal after the latest American strikes on its southern defence facilities. The US military said its forces struck Iranian missile and drone storage sites and coastal radar locations on Friday in response to an Iranian attack on a cargo ship transiting the strategic Strait of Hormuz. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
items.content: Iran on Saturday accused the United States of a "blatant violation" of the peace deal after the latest American strikes on its southern defence facilities. The US military said its forces struck Iranian missile and drone storage sites and coastal radar locations on Friday in response to an Iranian attack on a cargo ship transiting the strategic Strait of Hormuz. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

The US shot down three other drones aimed at the ship, he said. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Venezuela: Tens of thousands still missing after twin earthquakes
clusters.neutral_headline: Venezuelans dig for earthquake survivors as 72-hour rescue window nears end
bullet_summary: The death toll nearly doubled on Thursday to 920, but authorities warn the final number is expected to be far higher, with tens of thousands of people still reported missing
At least 920 people are confirmed dead and more than 51,000 missing after two quakes struck Venezuela on Wednesday
Satellite photos showed the destruction in Venezuela left by two powerful earthquakes that struck Wednesday and left more than 900 people dead
Rodríguez’s brother, Jorge, the president of the national assembly, said on Friday that the official number of dead had risen to 920

items.title: Venezuela: Tens of thousands still missing after twin earthquakes
items.standfirst: Foreign search and rescue teams have arrived in Venezuela to assist with the desperate and slow-moving search for survivors after the country's most powerful earthquake in more than a century. The death toll nearly doubled on Thursday to 920, but authorities warn the final number is expected to be far higher, with tens of thousands of people still reported missing. Frustration has also been growing over the official response and the limited resources available for rescue efforts.
items.content: Foreign search and rescue teams have arrived in Venezuela to assist with the desperate and slow-moving search for survivors after the country's most powerful earthquake in more than a century. The death toll nearly doubled on Thursday to 920, but authorities warn the final number is expected to be far higher, with tens of thousands of people still reported missing. Frustration has also been growing over the official response and the limited resources available for rescue efforts.

items.title: Race to find survivors as Venezuela's earthquake death toll soars
items.standfirst: The twin earthquakes that hit Venezuela killed more than 900 people, and that toll is likely to keep rising as frantic rescue and recovery operations ramp up.
items.content: The twin earthquakes that hit Venezuela killed more than 900 people, and that toll is likely to keep rising as frantic rescue and recovery operations ramp up.

items.title: Venezuela earthquake death toll tops 900 as rescuers race to find survivors
items.standfirst: The death toll of the Venezuelan earthquakes soared past 900 on Friday. But there is still time - one more day - in what's considered to be the "golden" window for finding survivors. Camilo Montoya-Galvez has the latest.
items.content: The death toll of the Venezuelan earthquakes soared past 900 on Friday. But there is still time - one more day - in what's considered to be the "golden" window for finding survivors. Camilo Montoya-Galvez has the latest.

items.title: What's needed on the ground in Venezuela after the devastating earthquakes
items.standfirst: Rescue teams in Venezuela are racing to dig through the rubble to locate thousands of people who are still missing after two earthquakes rattled the country. Fabio Biolchini, the head of operations for Latin America at Doctors Without Borders, joins "The Daily Report" with more on the immediate needs on the ground.
items.content: Rescue teams in Venezuela are racing to dig through the rubble to locate thousands of people who are still missing after two earthquakes rattled the country. Fabio Biolchini, the head of operations for Latin America at Doctors Without Borders, joins "The Daily Report" with more on the immediate needs on the ground.

items.title: Venezuela quake death toll reaches 920 as interim president vows to save ‘as many as possible’
items.standfirst: Delcy Rodríguez says foreign rescue teams are arriving as anger grows at official response and limited resourcesSurvivors tell of ‘brutal and fast’ Venezuela quake as hunt for survivors goes onVenezuela’s interim president, Delcy Rodríguez, has vowed to fight to save “as many people as possible” as the official death toll from the country’s worst earthquake in more than a century almost doubled, but frustration was growing at the perceived sluggishness of the government’s response.Rodríguez’s brother, Jorge, the president of the national assembly, said on Friday that the official number of dead had risen to 920. Delcy Rodríguez had earlier said almost 3,000 people were injured. Speaking during a tour of La Guaira, the most devastated region, she said foreign search and rescue groups were starting to arrive. Continue reading...
items.content: Delcy Rodríguez says foreign rescue teams are arriving as anger grows at official response and limited resourcesSurvivors tell of ‘brutal and fast’ Venezuela quake as hunt for survivors goes onVenezuela’s interim president, Delcy Rodríguez, has vowed to fight to save “as many people as possible” as the official death toll from the country’s worst earthquake in more than a century almost doubled, but frustration was growing at the perceived sluggishness of the government’s response.Rodríguez’s brother, Jorge, the president of the national assembly, said on Friday that the official number of dead had risen to 920. Delcy Rodríguez had earlier said almost 3,000 people were injured. Speaking during a tour of La Guaira, the most devastated region, she said foreign search and rescue groups were starting to arrive. Continue reading...

items.title: Venezuelans search for missing as earthquake death toll climbs
items.standfirst: The death toll from the twin earthquakes in Venezuela continued to rise on Friday, with at least 920 dead and more than 51,000 missing, as distress grew among Venezuelans. The 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude quakes that struck late Wednesday left a landscape of devastation, with countless collapsed buildings, particularly in La Guaira, a coastal town near Caracas, where residents are criticising the government's lack of action in rescue operations.
items.content: The death toll from the twin earthquakes in Venezuela continued to rise on Friday, with at least 920 dead and more than 51,000 missing, as distress grew among Venezuelans. The 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude quakes that struck late Wednesday left a landscape of devastation, with countless collapsed buildings, particularly in La Guaira, a coastal town near Caracas, where residents are criticising the government's lack of action in rescue operations.

items.title: Survivors tell of ‘brutal and fast’ Venezuela quake as hunt for survivors goes on
items.standfirst: Worst incident of its kind in country for more than 125 years leaves many searching for family members – and pleading for international helpVenezuela death toll doubles as interim president vows to save ‘as many people as possible’Nearly all of Ligia Level’s family lived in a trio of apartment blocks along Hotel Avenue, a seafront sweep of palm-specked resorts and high-rise condos along Venezuela’s Caribbean coast.When a powerful “doublet” of earthquakes jolted the region on Wednesday afternoon, those buildings and the lives within them came crashing down. Continue reading...
items.content: Worst incident of its kind in country for more than 125 years leaves many searching for family members – and pleading for international helpVenezuela death toll doubles as interim president vows to save ‘as many people as possible’Nearly all of Ligia Level’s family lived in a trio of apartment blocks along Hotel Avenue, a seafront sweep of palm-specked resorts and high-rise condos along Venezuela’s Caribbean coast.When a powerful “doublet” of earthquakes jolted the region on Wednesday afternoon, those buildings and the lives within them came crashing down. Continue reading...

items.standfirst: Massive search and rescue operations are underway in Venezuela on Friday as civilians and emergency workers rushed to find those trapped beneath the rubble more than a day after devastating twin earthquakes killed over 589 people.
items.content: Massive search and rescue operations are underway in Venezuela on Friday as civilians and emergency workers rushed to find those trapped beneath the rubble more than a day after devastating twin earthquakes killed over 589 people.

items.title: New US-Lebanon-Israel agreement signed
items.standfirst: The Israeli and Lebanese ambassadors announced an agreement on Friday that has been described as a step toward peace after months of cross-border fighting between Israeli forces and the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah. The deal marks a potential breakthrough in efforts to ease tensions following months of conflict. Camille Knight reports.
items.content: The Israeli and Lebanese ambassadors announced an agreement on Friday that has been described as a step toward peace after months of cross-border fighting between Israeli forces and the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah. The deal marks a potential breakthrough in efforts to ease tensions following months of conflict. Camille Knight reports.

items.title: Germany braces for record temperatures as heatwave moves east
items.standfirst: ​France, Britain, Switzerland and ‌Germany all experienced record heat in June and the weather system could test more records ⁠as it rolls across Germany towards Poland Saturday. Germany reached a new record of 41.3°C Friday near the city of Saarbruecken close to the French border while dozens of people, ⁠both young and old, died in France.
items.content: ​France, Britain, Switzerland and ‌Germany all experienced record heat in June and the weather system could test more records ⁠as it rolls across Germany towards Poland Saturday. Germany reached a new record of 41.3°C Friday near the city of Saarbruecken close to the French border while dozens of people, ⁠both young and old, died in France.

items.title: Moment woman pulled from rubble alive after Venezuela earthquake
items.standfirst: First responders and volunteers pulled a survivor from under the rubble of a collapsed building in La Guaira.
items.content: First responders and volunteers pulled a survivor from under the rubble of a collapsed building in La Guaira.

items.title: Race to find Venezuela quake survivors buried under rubble as death toll rises to 235
items.standfirst: Three children were among those pulled to safety in a miraculous rescue in La Guaira that aired on state TV.
items.content: Three children were among those pulled to safety in a miraculous rescue in La Guaira that aired on state TV.

clusters.title: Christmas market attacker jailed for life for murdering six in Germany
clusters.neutral_headline: Christmas market attacker jailed for life for murdering six in Germany
bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, Sky News World

items.title: Christmas market attacker jailed for life for murdering six in Germany
items.standfirst: A nine-year-old and five women were killed when Taleb Al-Abdulmohsen drove into the market in 2024.
items.content: A nine-year-old and five women were killed when Taleb Al-Abdulmohsen drove into the market in 2024.

items.title: Christmas market terrorist who drove into crowds jailed for life
items.standfirst: A man who killed six people when he drove into crowds at a Christmas market in Germany has been sentenced to life in prison.
items.content: A man who killed six people when he drove into crowds at a Christmas market in Germany has been sentenced to life in prison.
